title: World-first museum to bring new kind of AI to immersive art | AV Magazine
text:
World-first museum to bring new kind of AI to immersive art
By
AV Magazine
in
Display content
,
US&Canada
,
Visitor attractions
October 28, 2025
0
Refik Anadol’s Dataland, the first dedicated AI art museum, will host the first immersive works to use World Models, and will use scents from a large nature model, when it opens next spring.
Refik Anadol Studio’s Dataland, the world’s first museum of AI arts, has unveiled a preview of one of its five galleries.
The 2,300 sq m museum, which will redefine artistic expression for the age of AI, will open at The Grand LA, the Frank Gehry-designed cultural complex in downtown Los Angeles, next spring.
Gallery C – Infinity Room is based on Anadol’s first immersive data sculpture, which was created at University of California, Los Angeles in 2014.
The original Infinity Room was a 3.6m cube with mirrored walls, ceiling and floors that utilised projectors to display undulating pulses of black and white imagery, wielding light as a material and data as pigment.
Reflecting technical developments
Dataland’s Infinity Room reflects the technical and artistic developments the Refik Anadol Studio has made since then.
It will incorporate AI-generated scents from Large Nature Model, and is the first immersive space to use World Models, an advanced type of generative AI model that understands the dynamics of real-world physics and spatial properties.
As part of the museum’s mission to expand public understanding of AI and its potential to enhance human creativity, Dataland has launched its inaugural artist residency programme in partnership with Google Arts & Culture.
Over the course of six months, three selected artists will embark on projects that expand the creative horizons of human-machine collaborations. Residents will receive mentorship, funding and access to advanced technical resources.
The residencies will culminate in a public showcase at Dataland featuring immersive installations, artist talks and lecture series examining the evolving role of AI in art and culture.
